response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system reactions to proteins found in peanuts  
These reactions can cause symptoms such as hives swelling difficulty breathing  
Living underwater is not related to these symptoms  
Peanut allergies do not affect the ability to live in water  
The environment underwater does not contain peanut proteins  
Therefore peanut allergies do not prevent someone from living underwater  

Sec. 2  
The question may be based on a misunderstanding of allergies and their triggers  
Allergies are related to specific substances not to environmental conditions  
Living underwater does not involve exposure to peanut proteins  
People with peanut allergies can live in water as long as they avoid peanut exposure  
The severity of an allergic reaction depends on the amount of allergen ingested  
Therefore the inability to live underwater is not a consequence of peanut allergies
